metaTitle: Hollis Pest Control | Same-Day Service Queens NYC
metaDescription: Professional pest control in Hollis, Queens. Serving homes near Jamaica Ave & 193rd St. Rodents, roaches, bed bugs. Same-day service. Call now!
introParagraph: Hollis residents near Jamaica Avenue and 193rd Street trust The Best Pest Control NYC for fast, effective extermination services. Our technicians serve the tree-lined blocks between Hillside Avenue and the LIRR Hollis Station, protecting single-family homes, multi-family properties, and businesses along Francis Lewis Boulevard. Whether you're battling rodents, cockroaches, or bed bugs, we offer same-day appointments and lasting solutions tailored to Hollis's unique residential neighborhoods and commercial corridors.
whyThisAreaSection: Hollis faces persistent pest pressure due to its mature housing stock, much dating to the early-to-mid 20th century, creating entry points through aging foundations and siding. Proximity to the LIRR Hollis Station and busy transit corridors like Jamaica Avenue facilitates pest movement from commercial areas into residential blocks. Dense tree canopy along quiet streets attracts squirrels and raccoons, while nearby Hollis Park and older sewer infrastructure support rodent populations. The mix of detached homes with basements and attached properties creates ideal conditions for mice, rats, and insects year-round.
propertyTypesSection: Hollis is dominated by single-family detached and semi-detached homes with basements, crawl spaces, and attics—prime nesting sites for rodents and wildlife. Older properties feature foundation cracks, unscreened vents, and gaps around utility lines that welcome mice and rats. Multi-family homes and row houses share common walls, allowing cockroaches and bed bugs to spread between units. Brick and wood-frame construction shows wear over decades, while unfinished basements and cluttered storage areas provide shelter. Commercial properties along Jamaica Avenue face continuous pest pressure from foot traffic and adjacent residential zones.
commonPestsSection: Hollis homeowners most frequently encounter mice and Norway rats entering through basement gaps, garage doors, and foundation cracks, especially in fall and winter. German cockroaches thrive in kitchens and bathrooms of older multi-family buildings. Bed bugs spread through shared walls and second-hand furniture. Carpenter ants target wooden porch posts and siding, while squirrels and raccoons nest in attics and chimneys. Seasonal mosquitoes breed in clogged gutters and yard debris, and wasps build nests under eaves during warmer months.

faqs.question: How much does pest control cost in Hollis?
faqs.answer: General pest control in Hollis ranges from $175–$300 per treatment. Cockroach or ant extermination costs $350–$550, while mouse or rat removal runs $350–$600. Bed bug heat treatments range from $1,200–$3,500, and rodent exclusion work costs $500–$3,000 depending on home size and entry points.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a pest emergency in Hollis?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day pest control service throughout Hollis, including areas near Hollis Station and Jamaica Avenue. For urgent issues like rodent infestations, wasp nests, or severe cockroach problems, our technicians typically arrive within hours. Emergency service ranges from $350–$1,000 depending on the pest type and severity.

faqs.question: Are your treatments safe for children and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products applied according to strict safety protocols. For homes with children and pets near Hollis Park or residential blocks, we offer low-toxicity and non-chemical options like traps, exclusion, and heat treatments. Our technicians explain all precautions, and most families can remain home during treatment.

faqs.question: Why do I have mice every winter in my Hollis home?
faqs.answer: Hollis's older single-family homes have basement foundation cracks, gaps around utility pipes, and weathered door sweeps that mice exploit when temperatures drop. Garages with direct house access and cluttered basements provide shelter and nesting materials. We provide comprehensive rodent exclusion ($500–$3,000) sealing all entry points to prevent seasonal re-infestation.

faqs.question: How do bed bugs spread in Hollis multi-family homes?
faqs.answer: In Hollis's semi-detached and row houses, bed bugs travel through shared walls via electrical outlets, pipe chases, and baseboards. They also spread when residents move furniture between units or bring infested items from nearby transit areas. Multi-unit treatments are essential to prevent re-infestation from adjacent apartments.

faqs.question: Do you service commercial properties along Jamaica Avenue?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We provide comprehensive pest management for Hollis businesses, restaurants, and retail shops along Jamaica Avenue and Francis Lewis Boulevard. Commercial services include routine inspections, rodent control, cockroach treatments, and exclusion work tailored to storefronts and mixed-use buildings. Pricing varies based on square footage and pest pressure.
